source.note: Unit 2 Grammar 1 (Have to, planner pp.76-79) and Grammar 2 (Would like, planner pp.82-83). Grammar Workbook answer key pages 8-9 and Workbook answer key pages 16-17 were also used for extra source-backed samples.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Obligation with have to
grammarPoints.rule: Use have to or has to + the base form of a verb to say that something is necessary or required. Use don't or doesn't + have to to say that something is not necessary.
grammarPoints.pattern: subject + have to / has to + base verb

chart.title: Obligation with have to

intro_examples.text: I have to water the vegetables every day.
intro_examples.jp: 私は毎日野菜に水をやらなければなりません。

intro_examples.text: She has to plant the tomatoes in a sunny place.
intro_examples.jp: 彼女は日当たりの良い場所にトマトを植えなければなりません。

intro_examples.text: I don't have to buy any more seeds.
intro_examples.jp: 私はこれ以上種を買う必要はありません。

intro_examples.text: She doesn't have to weed today.
intro_examples.jp: 彼女は今日、草取りをする必要はありません。

rows.example: I have to water the vegetables every day.
rows.jp: 主語が I, you, we, they のときは have to を使います。

rows.example: She has to plant the tomatoes in a sunny place.
rows.jp: 主語が he, she, it のときは has to を使います。

rows.pattern: subject + don't have to + base verb
rows.example: I don't have to buy any more seeds.
rows.jp: 〜する必要がないときは don't have to を使います。

rows.pattern: subject + doesn't have to + base verb
rows.example: She doesn't have to weed today.
rows.jp: 三人称単数の主語には doesn't have to を使います。

chart.note_rule: We use have/has to + base form of a verb to talk about rules and responsibilities.
chart.note_exception: Use don't or doesn't before have to for things that are not necessary.
chart.note_exception_detail: Remember that has to changes to don't/doesn't have to in the negative. Never say doesn't has to.

table.title: Have to & Don't have to

cells: the tomatoes in a sunny place.

notes: Use have to / has to to describe rules and duties.
Use don't have to / doesn't have to when there is no obligation.

tab1_samples.text: I have to water the vegetables every day.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は毎日野菜に水をやらなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: She has to plant the tomatoes in a sunny place.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女は日当たりの良い場所にトマトを植えなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: I don't have to buy any more seeds.
tab1_samples.jp: 私はこれ以上種を買う必要はありません。

tab1_samples.text: She doesn't have to weed today.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女は今日、草取りをする必要はありません。

tab1_samples.text: The tomatoes are ripe. She has to pick them.
tab1_samples.jp: トマトが実っています。彼女はそれらを収穫しなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: I don't have to water the green beans when it rains.
tab1_samples.jp: 雨が降っているときは、インゲンマメに水をやる必要はありません。

tab1_samples.text: My father has to weed the garden so the vegetables can grow.
tab1_samples.jp: 野菜が育つように、父は庭の草取りをしなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: Farmers have to use a lot of water for their plants.
tab1_samples.jp: 農家は植物のためにたくさんの水を使わなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: Marisa has to feed and walk the dog every Saturday.
tab1_samples.jp: マリサは毎週土曜日に犬に餌をやり、散歩させなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: Ari has to water the garden on weekends.
tab1_samples.jp: アリは週末に庭に水をやらなければなりません。

rules.title: Use has to for third person singular
rules.jp_title: 三人称単数には has to を使う
rules.subtitle: Use has to with he, she, my brother, my mom, or my father.
rules.jp_subtitle: he, she や単数の名詞が主語のときは has to を使います。

transforms.from: I have to dig the soil.
transforms.to: He has to dig the soil.

transforms.from: They have to weed the garden.
transforms.to: Luisa has to weed the garden.

examples.text: My brother is stronger than me, so he has to dig.
examples.jp: 私の兄は私より力が強いので、彼が掘らなければなりません。

examples.text: My mom has to water the plants at home.
examples.jp: お母さんは家で植物に水をやらなければなりません。

rules.title: Doesn't have to for negative singular
rules.jp_title: 三人称単数の否定は doesn't have to
rules.subtitle: Use doesn't have to for singular third person. The verb has changes back to have.
rules.jp_subtitle: 三人称単数の否定形では doesn't のあとに have to を使います。has にしないように注意しましょう。

transforms.to: He doesn't have to pick tomatoes this week.
Luisa doesn't have to weed today.

examples.text: He doesn't have to pick tomatoes this week.
examples.jp: 彼は今週、トマトを収穫する必要はありません。

examples.text: She doesn't have to work in the garden on Sunday.
examples.jp: 彼女は日曜日、庭で働く必要はありません。

rules.title: Questions with Do and Does
rules.jp_title: Do と Does を使った疑問文
rules.subtitle: Use Do/Does + subject + have to + verb to ask about obligations.
rules.jp_subtitle: 義務について尋ねるときは Do / Does + 主語 + have to + 動詞の原形 を使います。

transforms.from: You have to wash the dishes.
transforms.to: Do you have to wash the dishes after dinner?

transforms.from: She has to clean her room.
transforms.to: Does she have to clean her bedroom on Saturday?

examples.text: Do you have to feed your cat every day?
examples.jp: あなたは毎日猫に餌をやらなければなりませんか。

examples.text: What does he have to do in the morning?
examples.jp: 彼は朝、何をしなければなりませんか。

rules.title: Difference between have and have to
rules.jp_title: have と have to の違い
rules.subtitle: Have shows ownership. Have to shows responsibility or obligation.
rules.jp_subtitle: have は所有（持っていること）を表し、have to は責任や義務（しなければならないこと）を表します。

examples.text: I have a dog and I have to walk it every day.
examples.jp: 私は犬を飼っていて、毎日散歩させなければなりません。

examples.text: I have long hair so I have to comb it every day.
examples.jp: 私は髪が長いので、毎日髪をとかさなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: My brother is stronger than me, so he has to dig.
mixed_samples.jp: 私の兄は私より力が強いので、彼が掘らなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: My mom has to water the plants at home.
mixed_samples.jp: お母さんは家で植物に水をやらなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: He doesn't have to pick tomatoes this week.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼は今週、トマトを収穫する必要はありません。

mixed_samples.text: She doesn't have to work in the garden on Sunday.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼女は日曜日、庭で働く必要はありません。

mixed_samples.text: Do you have to feed your cat every day?
mixed_samples.jp: あなたは毎日猫に餌をやらなければなりませんか。

mixed_samples.text: What does he have to do in the morning?
mixed_samples.jp: 彼は朝、何をしなければなりませんか。

mixed_samples.text: I have a dog and I have to walk it every day.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は犬を飼っていて、毎日散歩させなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: I have long hair so I have to comb it every day.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は髪が長いので、毎日髪をとかさなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: Luisa has to weed the garden today.
mixed_samples.jp: ルイサは今日、庭の草取りをしなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: We have to help our grandmother go grocery shopping.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちは祖母が食料品の買い出しに行くのを手伝わなければなりません。

explanation.title: Use has to
explanation.body: Because tomatoes are ripe, she needs to pick them. She is third-person singular, so use has to.

tab3_quiz.jp: トマトが実っています。彼女はそれらを収穫しなければなりません。

stem: water the green beans when it rains.

explanation.title: No obligation when it rains
explanation.body: When it rains, watering is not necessary. For subject 'I', use don't have to.

tab3_quiz.jp: 雨が降っているときは、インゲンマメに水をやる必要はありません。

stem: My brother is stronger than me, so he

explanation.title: Singular has to
explanation.body: He is third-person singular, so use has to.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私の兄は私より力が強いので、彼が掘らなければなりません。

explanation.title: Plural subject have to
explanation.body: Farmers is plural, so we use have to.

tab3_quiz.jp: 農家はしばしば新しい種を買わなければなりません。

stem: weed the garden today because she is busy with homework.

explanation.title: Singular doesn't have to
explanation.body: Luisa is singular, and is busy, so she doesn't have to weed.

tab3_quiz.jp: ルイサは宿題で忙しいので、今日は庭の草取りをする必要はありません。

explanation.title: Question form
explanation.body: In questions with 'Do', always use have to.

tab3_quiz.jp: あなたは今夜、皿洗いをしなければなりませんか。

explanation.title: Does + have to
explanation.body: In questions starting with 'Does', the verb changes back to the base form have to.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は日曜日、庭に水をやらなければなりませんか。

stem: I have a pet hamster. I have to

explanation.title: Base verb after have to
explanation.body: We must use the base form of the verb after have to.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私はハムスターを飼っています。毎日餌をやらなければなりません。

stem: Marisa have to do on Saturdays?

explanation.title: Singular auxiliary does
explanation.body: Marisa is third-person singular, so use 'does' in questions.

tab3_quiz.jp: マリサは土曜日に何をしなければなりませんか。

stem: work hard in school to succeed.

explanation.title: Obligation for success
explanation.body: With 'We', we use have to.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私たちは成功するために学校で一生懸命勉強しなければなりません。

tab4_master.jp: 私は毎日野菜に水をやらなければなりません。

answers: She doesn't has to weed today.
She doesn't have to weed today.
She don't have to weed today.
She don't has to weed today.

explanation.title: Doesn't have to
explanation.body: In negative singular, use doesn't have to. Never say doesn't has to.

tab4_master.jp: 彼女は今日、草取りをする必要はありません。
彼女はトマトを植えなければなりません。

answers: I have to walk my dog.

explanation.title: Have vs Have to
explanation.body: I have to walk my dog shows obligation, while I have a dog shows ownership.

tab4_master.jp: 私は犬の散歩をさせなければなりません。
私はこれ以上種を買う必要はありません。

explanation.title: Farmers is plural
explanation.body: Farmers is plural, so we use have to.

tab4_master.jp: 農家はたくさんの水を使わなければなりません。
彼女は自分の部屋を掃除しなければなりませんか。

stem: Fill in the blank: My brother is stronger than me, so he

explanation.title: Third person singular has to
explanation.body: My brother is singular, so we use has to.

tab4_master.jp: 私の兄は私より力が強いので、彼が掘らなければなりません。
ルイサは庭の草取りをしなければなりません。

stem: Complete the sentence: I have long hair, so I

explanation.title: Obligation for personal care
explanation.body: With 'I', use have to.

tab4_master.jp: 私は髪が長いので、毎日髪をとかさなければなりません。

japanese.title: have to を使った義務
japanese.rule: 義務や責任について話すときは have to / has to + 動詞の原形を使います。する必要のないことには don't have to / doesn't have to を使います。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + have to / has to + 動詞の原形

examples.sentence: I have to water the vegetables every day.
She has to plant the tomatoes in a sunny place.
I don't have to buy any more seeds.
She doesn't have to weed today.
The tomatoes are ripe. She has to pick them.
I don't have to water the green beans when it rains.
My father has to weed the garden so the vegetables can grow.
Farmers have to use a lot of water for their plants.
Marisa has to feed and walk the dog every Saturday.
Ari has to water the garden on weekends.
My brother is stronger than me, so he has to dig.
My mom has to water the plants at home.
He doesn't have to pick tomatoes this week.
She doesn't have to work in the garden on Sunday.
Do you have to feed your cat every day?
What does he have to do in the morning?
I have a dog and I have to walk it every day.
I have long hair so I have to comb it every day.
Luisa has to weed the garden today.
We have to help our grandmother go grocery shopping.

source.note: Obligation vs lack of obligation grammar charts and activities built from the planner Unit 2 pages and Workbook answer keys.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Polite requests with would like
grammarPoints.rule: Use would like to politely ask for something or to order food. In conversation, use the contraction form 'd like.
grammarPoints.pattern: subject + would like / 'd like + noun / to-infinitive

chart.title: Polite requests with would like

intro_examples.text: What would you like for lunch?
intro_examples.jp: お昼ご飯に何がよろしいですか。

intro_examples.text: I’d like a bowl of vegetable soup.
intro_examples.jp: 野菜スープを1杯いただきたいです。

intro_examples.text: What would you like for breakfast?
intro_examples.jp: 朝ご飯に何がよろしいですか。
果物をいただきたいです。

rows.example: What would you like for lunch?
rows.jp: 注文や要望を尋ねるときは would you like を使います。
「〜がほしいです」と丁寧に言うときは 'd like を使います。
複数人の要望には We'd like を使います。
相手に勧める時は Would you like を使います。

chart.note_rule: We use would like to politely express wants. It is much more polite than 'I want'.
chart.note_exception: In spoken English, we almost always use the contraction: I'd, we'd, she'd, he'd.
chart.note_exception_detail: Write I would = I'd, we would = we'd. Always say 'please' at the end of polite requests.

table.title: Ordering Food
notes: Would like can be followed by a noun or a verb with 'to' (e.g., I'd like to order).
It is polite to answer 'Yes, please' or 'No, thanks'.

tab1_samples.text: What would you like for lunch?
tab1_samples.jp: お昼ご飯に何がよろしいですか。

tab1_samples.text: I’d like a bowl of vegetable soup.
tab1_samples.jp: 野菜スープを1杯いただきたいです。

tab1_samples.text: What would you like for breakfast?
tab1_samples.jp: 朝ご飯に何がよろしいですか。

tab1_samples.text: I’d like some fruit.
tab1_samples.jp: 果物をいただきたいです。

tab1_samples.text: I’d like some pumpkin soup and zucchini bread.
tab1_samples.jp: カボチャのスープとズッキーニのブレッドをいただきたいです。

tab1_samples.text: I’d like some chicken and rice, please.
tab1_samples.jp: チキンとライスをいただけますか。

tab1_samples.text: I usually order pie, but today I’d like some ice cream.
tab1_samples.jp: いつもはパイを注文しますが、今日はアイスクリームをいただきたいです。

tab1_samples.text: Oh, and we’d like some bread, please!
tab1_samples.jp: あ、それとパンをいただけますか。

tab1_samples.text: Would you like to go to the garden?
tab1_samples.jp: 庭に行きたいですか。

tab1_samples.text: What would you like to do?
tab1_samples.jp: あなたは何をしたいですか。

rules.title: Use contractions in speech
rules.jp_title: 話し言葉では短縮形を使う
rules.subtitle: Use I'd, we'd, he'd, she'd, they'd in conversation.
rules.jp_subtitle: 会話では I'd, we'd などの短縮形を使うのが自然です。

examples.text: I'd like to plant seeds in the garden.
examples.jp: 庭に種を植えたいです。
私たちはぜひそうしたいです！

rules.title: Would like with 'to' + verb
rules.jp_title: would like + to + 動詞の原形
rules.subtitle: You can follow would like with a noun phrase or a to-infinitive.
rules.jp_subtitle: would like の後ろには名詞だけでなく、to + 動詞の原形を続けることもできます。

transforms.to: I'd like to pick some oranges.
We'd like to make some orange juice.

examples.text: What would grandma like to do?
examples.jp: おばあちゃんは何をしたいですか。

examples.text: Grandma would like to water the garden.
examples.jp: おばあちゃんは庭に水をやりたいです。

rules.title: Polite offers with Would you like...?
rules.jp_title: Would you like...? を使った丁寧な提案
rules.subtitle: Use Would you like + noun / to-verb? to offer something politely.
rules.jp_subtitle: 何かを丁寧に勧めるときは Would you like + 名詞 / to + 動詞 を使います。

transforms.from: Do you want to pick oranges?
transforms.to: Would you like to pick some oranges?

examples.text: Would you like a ride to school?
examples.jp: 学校まで車に乗っていきますか。

examples.text: Would you like to make some orange juice?
examples.jp: オレンジジュースを作りたいですか。

rules.title: Answering polite questions
rules.jp_title: 丁寧な質問への答え方
rules.subtitle: Answer 'Yes, please' or 'No, thanks' to be polite.
rules.jp_subtitle: 提案に対しては Yes, please（はい、お願いします）や No, thanks（いいえ、結構です）と答えるのがマナーです。

transforms.from: No, I do not want it.

examples.text: Would you like some water? Yes, please.
examples.jp: お水はいかがですか。はい、お願いします。

examples.text: Would you like a banana? No, thanks.
examples.jp: バナナはいかがですか。いいえ、結構です。

mixed_samples.text: I'd like to plant seeds in the garden.
mixed_samples.jp: 庭に種を植えたいです。

mixed_samples.text: We'd like that very much!
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちはぜひそうしたいです！

mixed_samples.text: What would grandma like to do?
mixed_samples.jp: おばあちゃんは何をしたいですか。

mixed_samples.text: Grandma would like to water the garden.
mixed_samples.jp: おばあちゃんは庭に水をやりたいです。

mixed_samples.text: Would you like a ride to school?
mixed_samples.jp: 学校まで車に乗っていきますか。

mixed_samples.text: Would you like to make some orange juice?
mixed_samples.jp: オレンジジュースを作りたいですか。

mixed_samples.text: Would you like some water? Yes, please.
mixed_samples.jp: お水はいかがですか。はい、お願いします。

mixed_samples.text: Would you like a banana? No, thanks.
mixed_samples.jp: バナナはいかがですか。いいえ、結構です。

mixed_samples.text: I'd like to go to the park.
mixed_samples.jp: 公園に行きたいです。

mixed_samples.text: He'd like to play soccer with his friends.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼は友達とサッカーをしたいです。

explanation.title: Polite question
explanation.body: In a restaurant, waiters use 'What would you like to order?' to be polite.

tab3_quiz.jp: マリオのカフェへようこそ。本日は何をご注文されますか。

stem: some pumpkin soup and zucchini bread, please.

explanation.title: Contraction of would like
explanation.body: In spoken English, we use 'I'd like' instead of 'I like' to order food politely.

tab3_quiz.jp: カボチャのスープとズッキーニのブレッドをいただきたいです。

explanation.title: Plural request
explanation.body: For plural 'we', use 'we'd like' as a polite contraction.

tab3_quiz.jp: あ、それとパンをいただけますか。

explanation.title: Would like + to-infinitive
explanation.body: We follow 'would like' with a to-infinitive (to do) when expressing an action desire.

tab3_quiz.jp: おばあちゃんは何をしたいですか。

explanation.title: Would like + to-verb
explanation.body: Use 'to water' to complete the to-infinitive after 'would like'.

tab3_quiz.jp: おばあちゃんは庭に水をやりたいです。

explanation.title: Polite acceptance
explanation.body: The standard polite way to accept an offer in English is 'Yes, please'.

tab3_quiz.jp: お水はいかがですか。はい、お願いします。

explanation.title: Polite rejection
explanation.body: The standard polite way to decline an offer in English is 'No, thanks'.

tab3_quiz.jp: パイはいかがですか。いいえ、結構です。

stem: I usually order pie, but today I

explanation.title: Contrast with usually
explanation.body: 'I usually order' is a habit, but 'today I'd like / I would like' represents a specific polite preference today.

tab3_quiz.jp: いつもはパイを注文しますが、今日はアイスクリームをいただきたいです。

explanation.title: Base form like
explanation.body: After the auxiliary 'would', we use the base form 'like'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼らは何を飲みたがっていますか。

explanation.title: Verb connection
explanation.body: When followed by another action, use the to-infinitive: They'd like to drink.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼らは炭酸飲料を飲みたがっています。

tab4_master.jp: 野菜スープを1杯いただきたいです。

explanation.title: Contraction meaning
explanation.body: 'I'd' in 'I'd like' stands for 'I would'.

tab4_master.jp: I'd は I would の短縮形です。
カボチャのスープとズッキーニのブレッドをいただきたいです。

stem: Waitress: What would you like to drink? Customer:

explanation.title: Polite restaurant response
explanation.body: Use 'I'd like...' to order politely in response to a waiter's question.

tab4_master.jp: オレンジジュースをお願いします。
朝ご飯に何がよろしいですか。

stem: Would you like to go to the garden?

explanation.title: Answering action offer
explanation.body: For 'Would you like to do something?', a polite action agreement like 'Yes, let's go!' or 'Yes, I'd like that' is used.

tab4_master.jp: 庭に行きたいですか。はい、行きましょう！
パンをいただけますか。

stem: Which is the most polite way to ask for a glass of water?
answers: I'd like a glass of water, please.
I want a glass of water.
Give me a glass of water.

explanation.title: Polite requests
explanation.body: Using 'I'd like ... please' is the standard polite request format.

tab4_master.jp: お水を1杯いただきたいのですが。

tab4_master.cue: Build what grandma wants to do.
tab4_master.jp: おばあちゃんは庭に水をやりたいです。

stem: Complete the sentence: My brother is strong, so he

explanation.title: Would like with singular
explanation.body: Singular subject 'he' matches with 'would like' to show preference.

tab4_master.jp: 弟は力強いので、手伝いたがっています。

japanese.title: would like を使った丁寧な表現
japanese.rule: 丁寧に注文したり、要望を言ったりするときに would like / 'd like + 名詞（または to + 動詞の原形）を使います。want よりずっと丁寧な表現です。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + would like / 'd like + 名詞 / to + 動詞の原形

examples.sentence: What would you like for lunch?
I’d like a bowl of vegetable soup.
What would you like for breakfast?
I’d like some fruit.
I’d like some pumpkin soup and zucchini bread.
I’d like some chicken and rice, please.
I usually order pie, but today I’d like some ice cream.
Oh, and we’d like some bread, please!
Would you like to go to the garden?
What would you like to do?
I'd like to plant seeds in the garden.
We'd like that very much!
What would grandma like to do?
Grandma would like to water the garden.
Would you like a ride to school?
Would you like to make some orange juice?
Would you like some water? Yes, please.
Would you like a banana? No, thanks.
I'd like to go to the park.
He'd like to play soccer with his friends.

source.note: Polite requests with would like and food ordering activities built from planner Unit 2 pages and Workbook answer keys.
